docs.title: Mouse Muscle Atlas (MMA)
docs.text: An ontology for mouse muscle and skeletal anatomy structures.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Mouse Muscle Atlas (MMA) is a comprehensive ontology containing 173 anatomical structures including:

  • Bones: Skull bones, ear ossicles, vertebrae, sternum, etc.
  • Muscles: Facial muscles, pharyngeal muscles, laryngeal muscles, etc.
  • Cartilage: Thyroid, arytenoid, cricoid cartilages, etc.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Hierarchy Tree – Interactive ontology browser
  • OBO Foundry

docs.text: Format Link OWL mma.owl OBO mma.obo JSON mma.json

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For questions or contributions, please open an issue on GitHub.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The Mouse Muscle Atlas (MMA) is an ontology designed to provide a standardized vocabulary for mouse muscle and skeletal anatomy structures. It enables precise annotation and data integration across research studies.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This ontology covers anatomical structures of the mouse head, neck, and upper body including:

docs.title: Skeletal Structures (Bones)
docs.text:
• Cranial bones (occipital, parietal, frontal, etc.)
  • Facial bones (maxilla, mandible, nasal, etc.)
  • Ear ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes)
  • Vertebrae, sternum, clavicle, scapula

docs.title: Cartilaginous Structures
docs.text:
• Laryngeal cartilages (thyroid, cricoid, arytenoid)
  • Alar cartilage

docs.title: Muscular Structures
docs.text:
• Facial expression muscles
  • Mastication muscles
  • Pharyngeal muscles
  • Laryngeal muscles
  • Hyoid muscles
  • Tongue muscles

docs.title: Imports
docs.text:
MMA imports from the following ontologies:

  • RO - Relations Ontology
  • BFO - Basic Formal Ontology
  • PATO - Phenotype and Trait Ontology
  • OMO - OBO Metadata Ontology
  • UBERON - Anatomy Ontology

docs.title: Development
docs.text: MMA is developed using the Ontology Development Kit (ODK).

docs.title: Contributing to MMA
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to the Mouse Muscle Atlas!

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text:
1. Check if the issue already exists
  2. Create a new issue with a clear description
  3. Use appropriate labels

docs.title: Requesting New Terms
docs.text:
To request a new anatomical term:

  1. Open a New Term Request
  2. Provide:
  3. Preferred term label
  4. Definition with citation
  5. Parent class suggestion
  6. Any relevant synonyms

docs.title: Making Changes
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Make your changes to src/ontology/mma-edit.owl
  4. Submit a pull request

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker
  • Git

docs.title: Running ODK Commands
docs.text:
cd src/ontology
sh run.sh make <target>

docs.title: Common Targets
docs.text: Command Description make test Run tests make prepare_release Prepare a release make refresh-imports Refresh all imports

docs.title: Editors Workflow
docs.text: This guide describes the standard workflow for editing the Mouse Muscle Atlas ontology.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Protégé installed
  • Git configured
  • Docker installed (for ODK commands)

docs.title: 1. Get the Latest Version
docs.text: git pull origin main

docs.title: 2. Create a Branch
docs.text: git checkout -b your-feature-branch

docs.title: 3. Edit in Protégé
docs.text:
1. Open src/ontology/mma-edit.owl in Protégé
  2. Make your changes
  3. Save the file

docs.title: 4. Run Quality Checks
docs.text:
cd src/ontology
sh run.sh make test

docs.title: 5. Commit and Push
docs.text:
git add src/ontology/mma-edit.owl
git commit -m "Add new terms for X"
git push origin your-feature-branch

docs.title: 6. Create Pull Request
docs.text: Create a pull request on GitHub and wait for automated checks to pass.

docs.title: Adding New Terms
docs.text:
When adding new terms, ensure you include:

  • Label: Human-readable name
  • Definition: Clear description with source citation
  • Parent class: Appropriate superclass
  • Synonyms: Alternative names (if applicable)

docs.title: ID Ranges
docs.text: Each editor should use their assigned ID range. Check src/ontology/mma-idranges.owl for your range.
